Combat sport events in 2023
ONE Fight Night 16: Haggerty vs. Andrade
The poster for ONE Fight Night 16: Haggerty vs. Andrade
PromotionONE Championship
DateNovember 4, 2023 (2023-11-04)
VenueLumpinee Boxing Stadium
CityBangkok, Thailand
Event chronology
ONE Friday Fights 39: Kongklai vs. Şen ONE Fight Night 16: Haggerty vs. Andrade ONE Friday Fights 40: Jaosuayai vs. Paidang

ONE Fight Night 16: Haggerty vs. Andrade was a combat sport event produced by ONE Championship that took place on November 4, 2023, at Lumpinee Boxing Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.[1][2]

Background

[edit]

A ONE Bantamweight Kickboxing World Championship for the vacant title between current ONE Bantamweight Muay Thai champion Jonathan Haggerty (also former ONE Flyweight Muay Thai champion) and current ONE Bantamweight champion Fabrício Andrade headlined the event. If successful, Haggerty and Andrade would have become the fourth fighter in ONE history to win a title in different sports (after Stamp Fairtex at ONE: Call to Greatness, Sam-A Gaiyanghadao at ONE: King of the Jungle and Regian Eersel at ONE on Prime Video 3).[1] The bout was originally scheduled to meet at ONE Fight Night 15, but the bout was moved to this event for unknown reasons.[3] Former champion Petchtanong Petchfergus was stripped of the title due to testing positive for both metenolone and boldenone, banned substance according to the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA).[4]

The inaugural ONE Welterweight Submission Grappling World Championship bout between Tye Ruotolo and Magomed Abdulkadirov served as the co-main event.[5][6]

During fight week, two bouts booked for this event suffered changes: Liam Nolan and Johan Estupiñan pulled out of his lightweight muay thai rematch against Sinsamut Klinmee and catchweight of 140 pounds muay thai against Saeksan Or. Kwanmuang for undisclosed reasons and was replaced by Mouhcine Chafi and Karim Bennoui respectively.[7]

At the weigh-ins, Mouhcine Chafi weighed in at 174.5 pounds, four and half pounds over the lightweight fight limit. His bout proceeded at catchweight and he was fined 30% of his purse, which went to his opponent Sinsamut Klinmee.[8]

Results

[edit]
Main card (Amazon Prime Video)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Bantamweight Kickboxing Jonathan Haggerty def. Fabrício Andrade KO (punches) 2 1:57 [a]
Welterweight Submission Grappling Tye Ruotolo def. Magomed Abdulkadirov Decision (unanimous) 1 10:00 [b]
Catchweight (140 lbs) Muay Thai Saeksan Or. Kwanmuang def. Karim Bennoui Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00
Lightweight Halil Amir def. Ahmed Mujtaba TKO (punches) 1 5:00
Lightweight Muay Thai Sinsamut Klinmee def. Mouhcine Chafi Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00 [c]
Strawweight Kickboxing Rui Botelho def. Zhang Peimian Decision (split) 3 3:00
Women's Strawweight Ayaka Miura def. Meng Bo Submission (scarf hold armlock) 1 2:09
Heavyweight Ben Tynan def. Kang Ji-won Submission (arm-triangle choke) 3 1:22
Women's Atomweight Kickboxing Cristina Morales def. Supergirl Jaroonsak TKO (punches) 1 2:54
Strawweight Lito Adiwang def. Jeremy Miado Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00
  1. ^ For the vacant ONE Bantamweight Kickboxing World Championship.
  2. ^ For the inaugural ONE Welterweight Submission Grappling World Championship.
  3. ^ Chafi missed weight (174.5 lb).

[9]

Bonus awards

[edit]

The following fighters were awarded bonuses:[10]

  • Performance of the Night ($100,000): Jonathan Haggerty
  • Performance of the Night ($50,000): Tye Ruotolo, Ben Tynan and Cristina Morales

Aftermath

[edit]

During the event post-fight interview with Jonathan Haggerty, Venum Training Camp's co-owner Mehdi Zatout and Alaverdi Ramazanov bypass security to stage confrontation with Haggerty, they breached security and forced themselves into the ring despite attempts by security to prevent them from doing so. It is unacceptable behaviour and jeopardises the safety of everyone in the stadium, make the CEO Chatri Sityodtong has suspended both Zatout and Ramazanov from attending events for 6 months.[11]
